paper_title: Biomarker Identification and Hydrogen Isotope Analysis

methods.method_name: GC-MS Analysis for Biomarker Identification and Quantification
methods.description: This method describes the process of using gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS) to identify and quantify saturated hydrocarbons in samples. It is used to separate and analyze volatile components based on their mass spectra and retention times.

steps.description: The saturated hydrocarbon fraction was analysed by gas chromatography -mass spectrometry (GC-MS) for identification and quantification.
GC-MS analysis was performed on a Shimadzu GCMS-QP2010 Ultra equipped with an AOC-20i auto sampler and a split-splitless injector operated in splitless mode.
steps.searchable_summary: perform GC-MS analysis using Shimadzu GCMS-QP2010 Ultra

steps.description: Volatile components were separated using a Zebron ZB-5HT Inferno GC column (30 m length, 0.25 mm inner diameter, 0.25 μm film thickness).
steps.searchable_summary: separate volatile components using Zebron ZB-5HT Inferno GC column

steps.description: The GC oven was programmed to heat with 20 °C min -1 starting at 60°C, to 180°C, and subsequently the temperature was ramped to 320 °C at 4 °C min -1 , followed by an isothermal phase of 30 min.
Helium was used as a carrier gas with a continuous flow rate of 1 ml min -1.
steps.searchable_summary: use helium as carrier gas at 1 ml/min

steps.description: The ion source of the MS operating system was set to 200 °C and the ionization energy at 70 eV.
steps.searchable_summary: set MS ion source to 200°C and ionization energy to 70 eV

steps.description: C 18 to C 33 n-alkanes were identified based on mass spectra from the literature and retention times.
steps.searchable_summary: identify n-alkanes using mass spectra and retention times

steps.description: Concentration of individual compounds was based on the comparison of peak areas relative to that of an internal standard (squalane) that was added to the samples before total lipid extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate compound concentrations using peak areas and internal standard

methods.method_name: GC-IRMS Analysis for Hydrogen Isotope Ratios
methods.description: This method involves using gas chromatography-isotope ratio monitoring-mass spectrometry (GC-IRMS) to determine hydrogen isotope ratios in samples. It is used to calibrate and measure isotopic compositions relative to a standard scale.

steps.description: Hydrogen isotope ratios were determined by gas chromatography-isotope ratio monitoring-mass spectrometry (GC-IRMS) with a Thermo Finnigan Delta XL mass spectrometer coupled to a Trace Ultra GC via a GC Isolink Conflo IV system.
steps.searchable_summary: determine hydrogen isotope ratios using GC-IRMS

steps.description: A standard mixture of n-alkanes with known isotopic composition (reference mixture A4, provided by Arndt Schimmelmann, Indiana University, USA) was run several times daily to calibrate the CO 2 reference gas against which the samples were measured.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate CO2 reference gas using standard n-alkane mixture

steps.description: All analyses were performed in triplicate and results are reported as the mean, and expressed relative to the VSMOW scale.
steps.searchable_summary: perform triplicate analyses and report mean relative to VSMOW
